name: youtube
description: Search YouTube for videos or retrieve trending YouTube content. Use ONLY for YouTube-specific requests. For general web searches use duckduckgo_search.
documentation: Searches YouTube for videos matching a query or retrieves the current trending videos, returning video metadata (title, channel, thumbnail, duration, view count, URL) rendered as an embedded card. Use exclusively for YouTube-specific requests — when the user asks for a YouTube video, wants to see what's trending on YouTube, or requests video content by topic. Common triggers: 'find me a video about X', 'show me lo-fi music', 'what's trending on YouTube', 'play a cooking tutorial', 'search YouTube for...', 'I want to watch something about...'. For general web searches unrelated to video content, use duckduckgo_search instead. Use result_count=1 for a single embedded video, result_count=3 for a browsable grid of results.

trigger.description: Invoked when the user asks about YouTube videos, trending content, or requests a video search.

YOUTUBE_API_KEY.description: YouTube Data API v3 key. Optional — falls back to scraping without it.

query.description: Search query. Leave empty to fetch trending videos.

result_count.description: Number of results to display: 1 (embed card) or 3 (thumbnail grid). Choose 1 for a specific video lookup, 3 for browse/discovery.

videos.description: Array of video objects with id, title, channel, thumbnail_url, duration, view_count, url.

tips: Ask for '1 result' or '3 results' to control the card layout.
Omit the query to see what's trending.
Configure a YouTube Data API v3 key in tool settings for better reliability (optional).
